---
title: "Data"
pagetitle: "Data — Nick Anderson"
output:
  html_document:
    includes:
      in_header: ["_head.html", "_meta_data.html"]
      after_body: _after_body.html
---

# Datasets

Every economist is responsible for their own data. Here is mine.

I am moving data hosting off Google Drive and onto self-hosted mirrors at **files.heterodata.org**. Each dataset below lists the self-hosted mirror as the primary location, with the existing Google Drive link preserved as a legacy fallback. Datasets under 1 GB are hosted whole; larger ones ship final outputs plus exact instructions for pulling the raw data from the original source. Programmatic access via the Robin API is planned for a future release.

---

## Underlying Data for Published Work on This Site

### *Duration and Public Finance in the United States, 2001–2023*
- **Self-hosted mirror:** [files.heterodata.org/duration-public-finance/](https://files.heterodata.org/duration-public-finance/)- [Google Drive (legacy)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1WYw1BDLebc86p4GOkAUdjdTOUOvrPhm5?usp=sharing)
- [Read the paper →](research.html#duration-and-public-finance)
- Interactive tool: [gerhard.heterodata.org](https://gerhard.heterodata.org)

### Tax Revenue Cyclicality
- **Self-hosted mirror:** [files.heterodata.org/tax-revenue-cyclicality/](https://files.heterodata.org/tax-revenue-cyclicality/)- [Google Drive (legacy)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1LZOTT-_jnL9rwczvfLr8AFgGP3hQKDaV?usp=sharing)
- Interactive tool: [gerhard.heterodata.org](https://gerhard.heterodata.org)

---

## HMDA — Home Mortgage Disclosure Act

The HMDA Explore dashboard at [hmdaexplore.com](https://hmdaexplore.com) is the public-facing version of this work. For HMDA, only the final aggregates are mirrored — the raw Loan/Application Register (LAR) is too large to host whole and must be pulled directly from the [CFPB](https://ffiec.cfpb.gov/data-browser/).

- **HMDA processing code (with data) — self-hosted mirror:** [files.heterodata.org/hmda-processing-code/](https://files.heterodata.org/hmda-processing-code/). Fair warning: this is not a perfect replication package; you will need to adjust file paths if you run the code, and the underlying files are large enough that a laptop may not have the RAM to process them in full.
  - [Google Drive (legacy)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1wX-A157XSy2lfMjoSgbpTU8s0PbH9Jyu?usp=sharing)
- **HMDA dashboard source code — self-hosted mirror:** [files.heterodata.org/hmda-dashboard-source/](https://files.heterodata.org/hmda-dashboard-source/)
  - [Google Drive (legacy)](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1w_3igaXfA0My0gK4gJpa0jg_d1KSxqff?usp=sharing)

---

## Public Banking Data Infrastructure

For programmatic access to bank regulatory data spanning 1863 to the present:

- **FreeNIC** — [github.com/andenick/FreeNIC](https://github.com/andenick/FreeNIC) — 1.5 billion rows, 217,210 institutions, 20 source datasets, 15 query tools. Draft public explorer: [freenic.org](https://freenic.org).

---

## Capitalism Replication and National Accounts

The replicated and extended data series behind the Anwar Shaikh *Capitalism* (2016) replication and the Shaikh & Tonak national-accounts work are published, with per-series provenance and downloads, at:

- [shaikh.heterodata.org](https://shaikh.heterodata.org) — the *Capitalism* replication and extended national-accounts datasets.

---

## Balance of Payments

International-accounts data — balance of payments, flow of funds, and cross-border value transfers across 258 countries — is explorable at:

- [lewis.heterodata.org](https://lewis.heterodata.org)

---

## Bank Failures and Balance Sheets

Bank-panel data on U.S. bank failures and balance sheets, 1865–2026, is explorable at:

- [volcker.org](https://volcker.org)

---

## Input–Output Accounts

The U.S. BEA Input-Output accounts and the Leontief inverse across sectors, 1997–2024:

- [leontief.heterodata.org](https://leontief.heterodata.org)

---

## Anwar Shaikh Papers

35 boxes / 77,452 pages / 532 GB of digital content at the Levy Economics Institute of Bard College. Public archive:

- [digitalcommons.bard.edu/as_archive](https://digitalcommons.bard.edu/as_archive/)
- Dedicated site: [realecon.org](https://realecon.org)

---
